archimate:Goal.name: Data Notification Deployment
archimate:Goal.documentation:
Definition: Architecture Decision Record from where you should specialise the ADR SBBs regarding the Data Notification Deployment
Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022
Source reference: https://www.iso.org/standard/74393.html
Additional information: The Data Notification Deployment ADR is a decision made in IT architecture that involves the deployment of notifications related to data. This concept is used to ensure that users are informed about changes or updates to data that they are interested in. The notifications can be sent via email, SMS, or other communication channels. The purpose of this concept is to improve data management and ensure that users are aware of any changes that may affect their work. This concept is particularly important in organizations that deal with large amounts of data and have many users accessing that data. By deploying data notifications, organizations can improve data security, reduce errors, and increase productivity.
Example: Decision: Data Notification capability is operated by the Data Exchanger (shared resources). Consequence: Data Subscription required
LOST view: Digital Solution Architecture Decisions Catalogue view
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataNotificationDeploymentGoal
ABB name: eira:DataNotificationDeploymentGoal
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: ADR-20230515180947459
Name: Architecture Decision Record about Data Notification Deployment
eira:adr_context: The context explains why we need to make a decision. It also describes the alternatives along with the pros and cons.
eira:adr_decision: The decision describes the justification for why the particular solution was accepted. It has more emphasis on the why rather than the how.
eira:adr_status: [Proposed (under review)|Accepted (approved and ready for implementation)|Superseded (superseded by another decision)]
eira:adr_consecuences: The consequences section contains information about the overall impact of an architectural decision. Every decision has trade-offs. That’s why it’s crucial to include the analysis to provide a clear picture.
Interoperability Layer: N/A
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DigitalSolutionArchitectureDecisionGoal

properties.value: Architecture Decision Record about Data Notification Deployment
Decision: Data Notification capability is operated by the Data Exchanger (shared resources). Consequence: Data Subscription required
The context explains why we need to make a decision. It also describes the alternatives along with the pros and cons.
The decision describes the justification for why the particular solution was accepted. It has more emphasis on the why rather than the how.
[Proposed (under review)|Accepted (approved and ready for implementation)|Superseded (superseded by another decision)]

The consequences section contains information about the overall impact of an architectural decision. Every decision has trade-offs. That’s why it’s crucial to include the analysis to provide a clear picture.

The Data Notification Deployment ADR is a decision made in IT architecture that involves the deployment of notifications related to data. This concept is used to ensure that users are informed about changes or updates to data that they are interested in. The notifications can be sent via email, SMS, or other communication channels. The purpose of this concept is to improve data management and ensure that users are aware of any changes that may affect their work. This concept is particularly important in organizations that deal with large amounts of data and have many users accessing that data. By deploying data notifications, organizations can improve data security, reduce errors, and increase productivity.
Architecture Decision Record from where you should specialise the ADR SBBs regarding the Data Notification Deployment
Digital Solution Architecture Decisions Catalogue view
